proposal_text: This research proposes to deploy a machine learning sepsis prediction algorithm across NHS England hospital trusts to identify patients at risk of sepsis deterioration in emergency department and ward settings. The algorithm uses physiological parameters including respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, temperature, systolic blood pressure, pulse rate, and level of consciousness — as recorded in the National Early Warning Score 2 (NEWS2) framework — alongside laboratory values and electronic health record data, to generate real-time risk scores for clinical staff. The training dataset was drawn predominantly from NHS patient records which have documented under-representation of Black, Asian, and minority ethnic patient populations in specialist care pathways. Academic analysis has found that vital signs used as predictive features are racially skewed, as minority patients may exhibit different baseline physiological profiles than the majority-white training population. The algorithm has not undergone prospective demographic bias auditing across ethnic subgroups prior to nationwide deployment. Clinical staff will receive risk scores without visibility into the algorithm's feature weighting or demographic performance variance. No formal mechanism for clinicians to record or escalate concerns about demographic performance disparities is included in the deployment framework.

assessment.risk_summary: The proposed sepsis prediction algorithm deployment involves high-risk factors such as processing of special category data about vulnerable groups, predictive policing, and autonomous systems making consequential decisions. The algorithm's lack of prospective demographic bias auditing and transparency in feature weighting and performance variance also pose significant risks.

identified_risks.risk: Processing of special category data without explicit consent
identified_risks.explanation: The algorithm uses physiological parameters, laboratory values, and electronic health record data, which may contain sensitive information about vulnerable groups.
The algorithm generates real-time risk scores for clinical staff, who will rely on these predictions to make life-or-death decisions.

identified_risks.risk: Lack of transparency in feature weighting and performance variance
identified_risks.explanation: Clinical staff will not have visibility into the algorithm's feature weighting or demographic performance variance, which may lead to biased decision-making.
The algorithm has not undergone prospective demographic bias auditing across ethnic subgroups prior to nationwide deployment.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Be fair and take action not to discriminate — consider and ameliorate biases the approach may perpetuate
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct prospective demographic bias auditing across ethnic subgroups

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consider and ameliorate biases in data and biased code/model outputs
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Regularly monitor and address any biases in the algorithm's performance

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Data protection by design and default
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Implement data protection measures to ensure transparency and accountability in data processing

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Respect privacy — obtain informed consent where required and protect personally identifiable information
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Obtain explicit consent from patients for the use of their data in the algorithm

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: The algorithm's lack of transparency and inadequate auditing may lead to biased decision-making, violating the right to non-discrimination
The processing of special category data without explicit consent may infringe on patients' right to private life

historical_precedents.lesson: Regularly monitor and address any biases in the algorithm's performance

historical_precedents.incident_title: Detroit Police Facial Recognition False Arrest
historical_precedents.lesson: Implement data protection measures to ensure transparency and accountability in data processing

Regularly monitor and address any biases in the algorithm's performance
Implement data protection measures to ensure transparency and accountability in data processing

tier1_mandatory_actions: Conduct prospective demographic bias auditing across ethnic subgroups
Regularly monitor and address any biases in the algorithm's performance
tier2_reflective_prompts: How will you ensure that the algorithm is transparent and accountable in its decision-making?
What measures will you take to prevent biased decision-making?
